items.title: Chunker losslessness
items.scenario: Your `chunk_text(text)` should partition any string such that joining the chunks recovers the original.
items.explanation: Property: `''.join(chunk_text(text)) == text` for all `text`. Hypothesis finds zero-width spaces, emoji, surrogate pairs, NULs — the exact edge cases that broke production last quarter.

items.title: JSON round-trip
items.scenario: Your `to_json(obj) / from_json(s)` pair must round-trip for any object in your domain.
items.explanation: Property: `from_json(to_json(obj)) == obj` for all `obj`. Catches encoder/decoder mismatches, key ordering bugs, NaN handling, integer overflow.

items.title: Retrieval top-k invariant
items.scenario: `top_k(query, k)` must return at most `k` results, all distinct, sorted desc by score.
items.explanation: Three properties: `len(out) <= k`, `len(set(d.id for d in out)) == len(out)`, `out == sorted(out, key=lambda d: -d.score)`. Property test runs against 100 random queries; any single break is reported with the smallest failing query.

items.title: Equivalence to a slow reference
items.scenario: You've written a fast vector ranker; you also have a slow brute-force reference.
items.explanation: Property: for all `(docs, query)`, `fast_rank(docs, query) == brute_force_rank(docs, query)`. Any disagreement is a real bug, with a shrunk minimum input.

items.title: Idempotent normaliser
items.scenario: `normalise_whitespace(s)` should be idempotent — applying it twice equals applying it once.
items.explanation: Property: `normalise(normalise(s)) == normalise(s)` for all strings. Cheap, devastatingly useful — a normaliser that isn't idempotent is silently building up errors over repeated processing.
